  have h₁ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) ((⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖)) pbar.outerParams.1 := by
    have h₂ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫) (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫) pbar.outerParams.1 := by
      -- Prove that the derivative of ⟨w, rotM₂φ P⟩ with respect to φ is ⟨w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟩
      have h₃ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => pbar.rotM₂ φ x) (pbar.rotM₂φ P) pbar.outerParams.1 := by
        sorry
      have h₄ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫) (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫) pbar.outerParams.1 := by
        -- Use the fact that the inner product is linear in the second argument
        have h₅ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => pbar.rotM₂ φ x) (pbar.rotM₂φ P) pbar.outerParams.1 := h₃
        have h₆ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫) (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫) pbar.outerParams.1 := by
          -- Apply the inner product to the derivative
          convert HasDerivAt.inner (hasDerivAt_const _ (pc.w)) h₅ using 1 <;>
            simp [inner, Matrix.dotProduct, LinearMap.add_apply]
          <;>
          simp_all [pbarrotM₂]
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Use the fact that the derivative of a constant times a function is the constant times the derivative
    have h₅ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams.1 := by

        field_simp [pc.norm_S_ne_zero] <;>
        ring_nf <;>
        simp_all
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h₅
  have h₂ : (nth_partial 1 (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams : ℝ) = ⇑(⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) := by
    -- Prove that the nth partial derivative is the same as the derivative at the point
    have h₃ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams.1 := h₁
    have h₄ : (nth_partial 1 (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams : ℝ) = ⇑(⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) := by
      -- Use the fact that the derivative is unique
      have h₅ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams.1 := h₁
      have h₆ : deriv (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams.1 = ⇑(⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) := by

          apply HasDerivAt.deriv
          exact h₅
        exact h₇
      -- Use the fact that the derivative is unique
      simp_all [nth_partial, Function.comp]
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h₄
  have h₃ : ‖P‖ * (nth_partial 1 (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams : ℝ) = ⟪pbar.rotM₂φ P, pc.w⟫ := by

    have h₅ : ‖P‖ * (nth_partial 1 (fun x : ℝ² => ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂ φ x⟫ / ‖P‖) pbar.outerParams : ℝ) = ‖P‖ * (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) := by

    have h₆ : ‖P‖ * (⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ / ‖P‖) = ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ := by

        simp_all [h₈]

        <;>
        ring_nf
        <;>
        simp_all [h₇]
        <;>
        aesop
    have h₇ : ⟪pc.w, pbar.rotM₂φ P⟫ = ⟪pbar.rotM₂φ P, pc.w⟫ := by

          simp [norm_eq_zero] at h ⊢
          <;> aesop
        simp_all [h4, GlobalTheoremPrecondition.fu_outer]
        <;>
        (try contradiction) <;>
        (try simp_all [norm_eq_zero]) <;>
        (try norm_num)
      · exact h
    field_simp [h3]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp_all [inner_mul_right]
    <;> field_simp [h3]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp_all [inner_mul_right]
  exact h1
EXCEPTION: TimeoutError:
sorry
